title: Make preset naming deterministic and keep preset browsing on one folder
summary: 0.4.7 replaces the failed inline preset-name field with a deterministic preset-name dialog backed by VaM's file browser and rooted to the player preset folder. The same folder now drives `Select Existing...`, favorites, and save/load behavior, so the player preset surface stops splitting naming and selection across different seams.
reasoning: The 0.4.6 direction was correct about matching the VaM preset-manager pattern, but the live plugin panel still did not render a usable inline name field. Instead of stacking more fragile cloned-input work on top, this slice switches to a stronger operator seam: one obvious preset-name control that opens a text-entry dialog, plus one browse dialog rooted to the exact same preset directory the favorites list is reading.

changes.summary: Replaced the invisible inline preset-name attempt with an explicit `Preset Name...` control that opens VaM's text-entry file browser and seeds it with the current or selected preset name.
changes.reasoning: This restores a deterministic way to name presets in the live plugin panel without depending on a fragile cloned input row that never rendered correctly.

changes.summary: Locked both `Select Existing...` and the preset-name dialog to the same player preset root folder used by the favorites catalog.
changes.reasoning: Browse, favorites, save, and load should all operate on one preset set instead of making the operator hop between mismatched locations.

changes.summary: Kept `Create New Preset` interactive even before a name exists so it can launch the naming dialog rather than dead-ending in a disabled state.
changes.reasoning: The save button is now the correct entry point for creating a new preset from scratch.

verification: Compile the CUA player in Release with the deterministic preset browse and naming dialogs.
Compile the no-input seam with `EnablePlayerInputFeatures=false` to `bin\\ReleaseNoInput\\fa_cua_player.dll`.
Build and deploy the versioned `fa_cua_player.0.4.7.dll` and `fa_player_asset.0.4.7.assetbundle` through the player foundation script.
Run foundation release validation for 0.4.7 and keep the generated receipt beside the release shelf.
knownIssues: This slice intentionally stabilizes core preset creation and selection first; it does not yet add defaults-management buttons or delete and rename workflows.
The player preset section still lives within the standard plugin two-column panel rather than a fully custom full-width preset-manager surface.
